name: From Brime de Urz to the Dolmen of San Adrián — Benavente y los Valles
name_original: De Brime de Urz al Dolmen de San Adrián — Benavente y los Valles
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Brime De Urz traverse the scenic Vidriales Valley, characterized by gentle hills and rural landscapes. The region features the Almucera River, providing riparian scenery, and extensive vineyards that contribute to the agricultural setting. Routes often include a mix of paved and unpaved segments, offering varied terrain for cyclists. This area in the Comarca de Benavente y los Valles provides a blend of natural beauty and historical sites. Best touring cycling routes around Brime De Urz The most popular touring cycling route is Castro de las Labradas and Alija del Infantado Loop, a 21.8 miles (35.0 km) trail that takes 2 hours 36 minutes to complete. This difficult route passes through historical landscapes and rural areas. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is The Benavente river vegas — Benavente, a moderate 39.1 miles (62.9 km) path. This route explores the river vegas, offering extensive views of the local waterways. Local touring cyclists also love the The Castle of the Counts of Benavente from Brime de Urz – circular through the Sierra de Carpurias, a 29.1 miles (46.8 km) trail leading through the Sierra de Carpurias, often completed in about 3 hours 14 minutes. Touring cycling around Brime De Urz is defined by gentle hills, the Almucera River, and vineyard landscapes. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various ability levels. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 5 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Brime De Urz's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Brime De Urz?
A: There are &lt;strong&gt;7&lt;/strong&gt; touring cycling routes available around Brime De Urz, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Brime De Urz?
A: The routes in Brime De Urz traverse the scenic Vidriales Valley, characterized by gentle hills and rural landscapes. You'll find a mix of paved and unpaved segments, with some routes exploring riparian scenery along the Almucera River and passing through extensive vineyards. Some routes may require good fitness due to unpaved sections or elevation gains.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Brime De Urz?
A: Yes, there is &lt;strong&gt;1 easy&lt;/strong&gt; touring cycling route available. While specific family-friendly routes are not detailed, easy routes with gentle terrain are generally suitable for those new to touring cycling or looking for a relaxed ride.

Q: Are there challenging touring cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Absolutely. For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1383963597/castro-de-las-labradas-and-alija-del-infantado-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro de las Labradas and Alija del Infantado Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult route covering 35.0 km (21.8 miles) with significant elevation changes, passing through historical landscapes and rural areas.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I see along touring cycling routes in Brime De Urz?
A: Many routes offer glimpses into the region's rich history. You can explore ancient settlements and historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5453005" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro de Las Labradas&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5966274" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen de San Adrián&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1037613425/the-castle-of-the-counts-of-benavente-from-brime-de-urz-circular-through-the-sierra-de-carpurias" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Castle of the Counts of Benavente from Brime de Urz – circular through the Sierra de Carpurias&lt;/a&gt; route will take you past the historic castle. Other notable attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5786385" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de los Pimentel&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5509742" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Santa Marta de Tera&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any natural features to explore while cycling in the area?
A: Yes, the region is rich in natural beauty. You can cycle alongside the Almucera River, known for its riparian landscapes, and through extensive vineyards that are economically important for the 'Valles de Benavente' designation. The 'Urz' in Brime de Urz refers to local heather, suggesting its presence in the natural environment. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1383275431/the-benavente-river-vegas-benavente" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Benavente river vegas — Benavente&lt;/a&gt; route specifically explores these riverine landscapes.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes starting from Brime De Urz?
A: Yes, a notable circular route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1037613425/the-castle-of-the-counts-of-benavente-from-brime-de-urz-circular-through-the-sierra-de-carpurias" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Castle of the Counts of Benavente from Brime de Urz – circular through the Sierra de Carpurias&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through the Sierra de Carpurias.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Brime De Urz?
A: While specific weather data isn't provided, regions like Brime De Urz in Zamora, Spain, typically offer pleasant cycling conditions during spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October) when temperatures are milder and the landscapes are vibrant. Summers can be hot, and winters cooler, so checking local weather forecasts is always recommended.

Q: Is there parking available near the touring cycling routes in Brime De Urz?
A: Brime De Urz is a rural area, and while specific parking facilities for each route are not detailed, you can generally find parking in or near the village center. For routes starting directly from Brime De Urz, look for suitable spots within the village or at designated trailheads if available.

Q: Can I find places to eat or drink along the cycling routes?
A: Brime De Urz and the surrounding towns in the Comarca de Benavente y los Valles offer local establishments. While not every route will have cafes directly on the path, you can find traditional bodegas (wineries) and local eateries in the villages you pass through, especially in larger towns like Benavente. It's advisable to carry snacks and water, especially on longer routes.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in Brime De Urz?
A: Touring cyclists often praise the blend of natural beauty and historical immersion. The serene Vidriales Valley, the picturesque Almucera River, and the extensive vineyards provide a tranquil backdrop. The opportunity to cycle through ancient landscapes, past Roman remains and medieval sites, adds a unique cultural dimension to the physical activity. The varied terrain, from gentle hills to more challenging sections, also caters to a wide range of abilities.

Q: Are there any routes that specifically visit the Dolmen of San Adrián?
A: Yes, the route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1384016629/from-brime-de-urz-to-the-dolmen-of-san-adrian-benavente-y-los-valles" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Brime de Urz to the Dolmen of San Adrián — Benavente y los Valles&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 14.4 km (8.9 miles) ride that takes you directly to this significant historical site.
tips: Ruins of a palace and castle built in the 15th century by members of the Escobar family, who were the lords of the town. Of the Palace, now in a state of abandonment, the outer masonry walls have been preserved. The castle is visible from the road.
A prehistoric stone circle. The structure was usually built from locally quarried granite or slate. They were used for various ceremonies throughout the seasons.
Brime de Urz is a small town south of the Sierra De Carpurias. The most notable monument in the city is the Ermita de San Esteban, which stands on top of a hill overlooking the city.

waypoints.name: Ermita de San Esteban de Brime de Urz
